project.name: Cisco Tango
project.description: Enterprise iOS indoor asset tracking app

overall_health.label: Needs Attention
overall_health.summary: Project is active and making progress toward GA, but 2 critical production bugs and a growing review bottleneck need immediate attention.
overall_health.breakdown: 2 of 5 dimensions are Red, 2 are Yellow, 1 is Green

velocity.why_this_status: Yellow because the backlog is growing 3.3x faster than completion. This is expected in pre-GA with active field testing, but sustained growth at this rate will become a problem.
velocity.detail: 15 items completed in the last 14 days while 50 new items were created. The creation spike comes from Northwestern Medicine beta testing feedback, HIMSS follow-ups, and Cisco Live learnings being translated into tickets.

quality.why_this_status: Red because there are 2 open critical bugs. Any critical bug that blocks a core user flow triggers Red status.

critical_items.summary: Login is pointing to Sandbox in PROD
critical_items.risk: Fix submitted -- now in Code Review. Blocks all production users until merged.
Reported by Lucas during multi-user onboarding at customer site

quality.detail: Both critical bugs were created on April 1. CT-485 (login sandbox) has been fixed by Paula and is now in Code Review -- needs urgent merge. CT-486 (venue freezing) still has no owner.

flow.why_this_status: Red because 11 items are queued for Code Review with only 2 active developers. The team reduction from 5 devs to 2 has made the review bottleneck worse. CT-485 (critical login fix) needs to be merged urgently.

stuck_items.context: Major architectural refactor: replacing unconventional bottom sheet pattern with Apple HIG-compliant behavior
Cleanup that depends on CT-433 being merged first

stuck_items.summary: Rename new objects removing the 'New' keyword
stuck_items.context: Sub-task of the bottom sheet work

flow.detail: The bottom sheet refactor (CT-433) is a deliberate architectural improvement -- the team is replacing a custom navigation pattern with native iOS behavior. The extended review time is partly expected for a change this large, but it's creating a queue.

communication.why_this_status: Green because all team members are active, thread engagement is high (conversations, not monologues), and bug reports get rapid responses.

positive_signals: App v1.0.1 launched on App Store (Mar 25) with team celebration
HIMSS 2026 report showed very positive customer reactions to demos
Successful demo day with important stakeholders attending
Navigation working well after on-site testing in San Francisco
AI-generated QA test case executed successfully with zero manual code
concern_signals: Multiple crash/login bug threads in the last 48 hours
Stale data issue at beta site required extended debugging
Pendo/Sentry credentials still pending from Cisco side
Team member (Gustavo) transitioning off project to AKQA opportunity

communication.detail: The Slack channel is healthy. Bug reports from Lee and Camila get threaded responses within minutes. Cross-functional participation is strong -- devs, QA, PM, design, and the client-side program manager are all actively communicating.

alignment.headline: Weekly syncs are consistent, but 5 action items have no Jira tickets

alignment.why_this_status: Yellow because 5 action items from the March 24 and March 31 meetings have no corresponding Jira tickets. These are commitments that could fall through the cracks.

untracked_action_items.action: Build custom analytics API (Pendo-compatible format) for event routing
Align with Bharath on business outcome metrics before building analytics dashboard
Schedule dedicated app onboarding meeting with Julian and Bharath
Schedule dedicated business KPIs meeting with Julian and Bharath
Monitor and share user testing feedback from Mandy (Figma prototype sessions)

key_decisions: Three red flags must be resolved before GA: login flow, building auto-detection, blank map onboarding
Email-first login model replacing three-button screen
Building auto-detection as primary experience (BLE beacon UID + GPS fallback)
Pendo is a stopgap -- move to custom analytics API
Internal employee testing postponed until red flags are fixed
alignment.detail: The team has strong meeting discipline -- weekly syncs are happening consistently and producing clear decisions. The gap is in follow-through: 5 action items discussed in meetings were not turned into trackable Jira tickets.

insights.title: Critical login fix in Code Review -- needs urgent merge
insights.detail: CT-485 (Login pointing to Sandbox in PROD) has been fixed by Paula and moved to Code Review. It needs to be merged ASAP to unblock production users. CT-486 (Venue screen freezing) is still unassigned.
insights.recommendation: Merge CT-485 today. Assign CT-486 to a developer. Both block GA readiness.

insights.title: Code Review queue doubled to 11 items -- only 2 devs to review
insights.detail: Code Review queue grew from 5 to 11 items overnight. CT-433 (14 days), CT-442 (13 days), CT-485 (critical login fix, just entered review). With only Paula and Vilson as active developers, the review capacity is severely constrained.
insights.recommendation: Consider bringing Joao back temporarily to help clear the review queue. Prioritize CT-485 (critical) and CT-433 (blocking downstream work).

insights.title: 5 meeting action items have no Jira tickets
insights.detail: Cross-referencing Notion meeting notes (Mar 24, Mar 31) against Jira found 5 commitments with no tracking ticket: analytics API build, KPI alignment, onboarding deep-dive, KPI meeting, user testing feedback.
insights.recommendation: Create Jira tickets for these to ensure they don't fall through the cracks before GA.

insights.title: Backlog growing 3.3x faster than completion rate
insights.detail: 50 items created vs 15 completed in 14 days. This spike comes from Northwestern beta feedback, HIMSS follow-ups, and Cisco Live learnings being translated into tickets.
insights.recommendation: Expected for this project phase. Focus the team on the 3 GA red flags rather than spreading across all new items.

insights.title: Team reduced significantly -- key resourcing risks ahead
insights.detail: Joao Limiere (Dev), Louise Bougleux (QA Lead), and Gustavo Stefani (Designer) are off the project. Bruno Duarte wrapping up this week. Francine and Camila reduced to 50%. Additionally, Paula (TL) has jury duty on 11 days in April, creating unpredictable availability for the most critical team member.
insights.recommendation: Joao Limiere was proposed as backup for Paula's jury duty days. Confirm his availability. Design gap needs coverage -- new design allocation planned for April.

insights.title: 91 days to GA -- quality over features from here on
insights.detail: Launch timeline: Cisco Live Vegas (May 31) -> General Availability (July 1) -> First tags ship (July 8) -> SOW end (July 21). Client direction: 'Between now and launch, our job is testing and polishing, not building new things.' The Nearby experience carries the entire product since Cisco is launching with a single UWB tag SKU.
insights.recommendation: Align sprint priorities with the 'quality over features' mandate. Nearby mode must be bulletproof.

red_flags.detail: Email-first login model agreed upon. Dnyanada has started work.
Auto-detection via BLE beacon UID + GPS fallback.
Structural UX problem. Needs onboarding redesign, not tooltips.

ga_readiness.blockers_for_internal_testing: All 3 red flags must be resolved before internal Cisco employee testing (confirmed by Vikas and Bharath on March 31).
